title: delivery_queue has no index on post_id; ON DELETE CASCADE absent causing orphan rows
description: db.rs:51: delivery_queue.post_id references posts(id) but has no ON DELETE CASCADE. SQLite with foreign keys disabled (the current state) means deleting a post leaves delivery_queue rows pointing to a non-existent post. In process_batch, the None branch (line 162-167) marks these dead individually, which is correct, but only if PRAGMA foreign_keys is OFF. More importantly, there is no index on delivery_queue(post_id), so if a manual admin delete of a post is ever implemented, the mark_dead scan is a full table scan. Additionally, the idx_delivery_pending index covers (status, next_retry) but NOT (post_id), so the post lookup in process_batch requires a JOIN which scans all delivery rows for a post. This is a latent schema deficiency that pairs with the missing foreign_keys pragma finding.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
